description: Early modern oceanic exploration must stay scoped to navigation, charting, and caravel-era ship operation, not conquest, banking finance, or mercantilist policy.

checks.reason: The node is anchored to the late-15th-century graph boundary for charted early modern European oceanic exploration rather than a single later voyage.
Caravel-era ship design enabled long-distance oceanic exploration without being the only possible vessel lineage.
Navigation charts and mapmaking enabled route recording and expansion.
Compass direction-finding enabled open-sea route navigation.
Astrolabe and quadrant instruments enabled celestial navigation and latitude estimation.
Gunpowder belongs to conquest or military-expansion scope, not direct oceanic route-finding.
Banking is expedition-finance context, not a direct operational prerequisite.
Mercantilism is later commercial policy context and should remain on trade or colonial-system nodes.
